turns.content: Eid is coming up and I have literally nothing to wear. need to sort an outfit and I'm overthinking it as usual
Eid outfit stress is real! What kind of vibe are you going for — traditional, modern, somewhere in between? And is this for Eid prayer, the family gathering, or both?

both tbh. I usually wear something that works for the prayer in the morning and then the big family dinner. so it needs to be modest enough for the mosque but also cute enough for photos loool. I was thinking a nice abaya or maybe a two-piece set? I'm stuck between going full traditional Somali dirac or doing something more modern

Both are gorgeous options! A few thoughts:

**Dirac route:** If you go traditional Somali, the colours and fabrics are honestly unmatched for Eid photos. You could do a bold colour — emerald green, deep burgundy, or even a rich gold — and pair it with a matching garbasaar. Very elegant, very statement.

**Modern route:** A structured two-piece set (wide-leg trousers + long tunic or a co-ord with a flowing top) can look very put-together. Brands like Aab, Inayah, and Modanisa have some beautiful Eid collections right now.

**Middle ground:** A modern abaya in a colour (not just black) with some embroidery or detail work. Wear it open over a coordinating outfit underneath. Works for prayer and looks gorgeous for the family dinner.

What colours do you tend to gravitate toward?

I love that middle ground idea actually. I wore a dirac last Eid so I want to switch it up. I'm thinking maybe a dusty pink or sage green? something soft. I wear so much black at work that I want something different for Eid

Dusty pink and sage green are both stunning choices for Eid — they photograph beautifully too. Sage green especially is having a moment right now and looks amazing against all skin tones.

For the abaya in that colour range, check out:

- **Aab Collection** — they do really nice coloured abayas with subtle embroidery, great quality
- **Inayah** — more minimalist, clean lines, lovely fabrics
- **Haute Hijab** — they sometimes have open-front abayas that drape really well
- **Sanzaa London** — smaller brand but beautiful craftsmanship

For the hijab styling, are you thinking matching the abaya or doing a contrast colour? A cream or off-white hijab with sage green is a really clean combination.

ooh cream with sage green yes that sounds so nice. I was also thinking about how to style the hijab differently for Eid. I usually just do a basic wrap for work but I want something a bit more for the occasion. any ideas?

Definitely — Eid is the time to go a bit extra with the hijab styling! Some ideas:

- **Pleated or textured fabric:** A crinkle chiffon or pleated hijab adds dimension without extra effort. The folds catch the light in photos.
- **Turban style wrap:** More structured, shows off earrings if you wear them, looks very polished. Works really well with an open abaya.
- **Side drape with a hijab pin or brooch:** Classic but elevated. A simple gold or pearl pin at the side can make a basic wrap look intentional.
- **Layered look:** A matching cap underneath with the hijab draped looser — gives volume and looks very elegant.

If you go sage green abaya with cream hijab, a gold hijab pin would tie it together perfectly. Do you usually wear jewellery with your Eid outfit?

I love the side drape with a brooch idea. and yeah I usually do gold jewellery for Eid — my mum gave me this beautiful gold necklace that I only bring out for special occasions. I think gold accessories with sage green and cream would be proper elegant

That sounds absolutely beautiful — sage green abaya, cream hijab with a side drape and gold brooch, your mum's gold necklace. Very elegant without being overdone. The necklace from your mum adds that sentimental touch too which is lovely for Eid.

For shoes, since you'll be at the mosque and then family dinner — something comfortable but polished. Gold or nude block heels, or if you want to be practical, pointed-toe flats in gold or cream. Saves you from being in pain by the evening.

Do you have the abaya yet or do you still need to buy it?

still need to buy it which is the stressful part loool. I might check Aab online tonight and if they don't have what I want I'll go into Manchester Arndale at the weekend — there's a couple of modest fashion shops in there that are decent. how long do online orders usually take from Aab?

Aab is UK-based so their standard delivery is usually 3-5 working days, and they sometimes have express options for next day. Around Eid they can get backed up though — everyone's ordering at the same time — so I'd order sooner rather than later if you go that route. If Eid is in a couple of weeks you should be fine with standard delivery, but don't leave it to the last minute.

The Arndale backup plan is solid too — trying things on in person means no returns stress and you know the fit works with your hijab style. Might be worth going in on a weekday evening if you can, weekends near Eid that place gets absolutely rammed.

yeah true it's gonna be madness on the weekend. maybe I'll try go after work one day this week. okay I think I've got a plan — sage green open abaya, cream hijab, gold accessories, comfortable shoes. wallah I'm actually excited now
That's a gorgeous outfit plan — you're going to look amazing. Go get that abaya sorted this week and you'll have one less thing to stress about. Eid Mubarak in advance!
oh wait actually what do I wear UNDERNEATH the open abaya? like do I go with a matching sage green dress or keep it simple with a white top and wide-leg trousers? I always overthink this part

For an open abaya the underneath outfit matters a lot since it'll show when the abaya falls open. Two solid options:

- **White or cream wide-leg trousers with a simple cream top** — this keeps the focus on the abaya itself and ties in with the cream hijab. Very clean, very cohesive.
- **Matching sage green dress or co-ord underneath** — this gives you the option to take the abaya off later if you get warm at the family dinner. You've basically got two outfits in one.

Honestly I'd go with the white/cream underneath. It's easier to put together, you probably already own the pieces, and it won't compete with the abaya. Save the mental energy for the hijab styling and accessories — that's where the impact is.
that's so true I literally have cream wide-legs already. one less thing to buy loool. okay NOW I actually have a full plan. you've been bare helpful with this, I'm going in with full confidence

That's what I like to hear! You've got the full vision sorted — sage green abaya, cream underneath, cream hijab with the side drape and gold brooch, your mum's necklace, gold or nude shoes. Chef's kiss of an Eid outfit. Go get that abaya this week and you're all set. Eid Mubarak in advance!
